hero.eyebrow: AN OPINIONATED JS COMPANION TO SPEC-KIT
hero.subtitle: Templates, guides, and Claude Code integration patterns for the JavaScript ecosystem — so you go from blank page to shipped feature without the usual blank-page paralysis.

showcase.heading: One file. Every assumption on the table.
showcase.para1: A spec isn't documentation after the fact. It's the contract you hand to Claude Code before any line gets written — the problem, the acceptance criteria, what's explicitly out of scope.
showcase.para2: It survives the refactor. It argues the trade-offs. It keeps the agent honest across sessions.

flow.heading: From intent to shipped — three files.
flow.intro: The spec states what and why. The plan argues how. The tasks list atomic steps each traceable to an acceptance criterion. No other artefact. No hidden slack. Claude Code reads the three and executes.
flow.specSub: Problem, audience, acceptance criteria, non-goals — written first, never skipped.
flow.planSub: Stack choices, data contracts, security posture. Every non-trivial decision justified in one sentence.
flow.tasksSub: Atomic, verifiable, each mapped to an AC. Completable in ≤ 30 minutes or split.

closing.heading: Stop arguing with a blank page.
closing.body: Copy the templates, write your first spec in twenty minutes, hand it to Claude Code. The site you're reading was built the same way — its specs live in /specs/mvp-launch/.
